raw_text:
DISCLAIMER
The material in this presentation has been prepared by LOW QEM CONSULTING QFZ LLC (hereafter referred to as “Low-Qem”) and is general background information
about Low-Qem’s activities current as at the date of this presentation. This information is given in summary form and does not purport to be complete. Information in this
presentation, including forecast financial information, should not be considered as advice, a recommendation or an offer to potential investors in relation to holding,
purchasing or selling securities or other financial products or instruments and does not take into account your particular investment objectives, financial situation or
needs. In particular, any statements included herein pertaining to potential investment returns (be they ‘targeted’ or otherwise) are hypothetical in nature and cannot in
any way be considered as a promise of actual returns or any other form of contractual offer or commitment by Low-Qem. Before acting on any information you should
consider the appropriateness of the information having regard to these matters, any relevant offer document and, in particular, you should seek independent financial
advice. All securities and financial product or instrument transactions involve risks, which include (among others) the risk of adverse or unanticipated market, financial or
political developments and, in international transactions, currency risk. This presentation may contain forward looking statements including statements regarding our
intent, belief or current aspirations with respect to Low-Qem’s businesses and operations, market conditions, results of operation and financial condition, capital
adequacy, specific provisions and risk management practices. Readers are cautioned not to place undue reliance on these forward-looking statements. Low-Qem does
not undertake any obligation to publicly release the result of any revisions to these forward-looking statements to reflect events or circumstances after the date hereof to
reflect the occurrence of unanticipated events. While due care has been used in the preparation of forecast information, actual results may vary in a materially positive or
negative manner. Forecasts and hypothetical examples are subject to uncertainty and contingencies outside Low-Qem’s control.
CONTACTS
Greg Bogie
Chief Executive
greg@low-qem.com
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only

raw_text:
About Us
Based in Doha, Low-Qem will be Qatar’s first battery materials plant, benefiting from the region's strategic location, industrial
infrastructure, and drive for economic diversification in the metals and chemicals space
Low-Qem will deploy world leading mineral processing technology to produce low-carbon materials that will supply the cathode
active materials (CAM) for rechargeable batteries used in electric vehicles and renewable energy storage in growth markets across
EMEA, North America and China
Low-Qem is offering a share in the platform growth of $800m+ annual 2-train plant revenue1 that will unlock a multi-product, multi-site
global expansion plan from its base in Qatar
Low-Qem will be operational in the Qatar Free Zone by 2030, corresponding with the Qatar National Vision 2030 initiative formed by the
Government of Qatar in 2008.
Low-Qem has a highly knowledgeable executive with over 20 years of complex project development and financing in the energy
transition globally
1 - based on steady state operation of 2-train lithium hydroxide refinery and long-term commodity forecasting
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only
3

raw_text:
Experienced Leadership
Greg—our Chief Executive—is an accomplished company executive with substantial experience in the
development, financing and execution of fast-growth strategies within regulated industries, covering
£50Bi in product value over a 20-year period.
His career spans oil & gas, new-build nuclear, chemical manufacturing, and semi-conductors involving
responsibilities with companies such as TotalEnergies, EDF Energy, Google, Intel and most recently with
Green Lithium.
Greg is himself dynamic and very results-driven with a proven track record in fast-paced
commercialization and growth. Skilled at leveraging commercial outcomes to unlock pools of capital,
Greg also possesses a strong understanding of global commodity markets, complex project execution,
intellectual property & licensing, and strategic business development.
Another of Greg’s key strengths is in fostering lasting relationships with stakeholders across various
geographies. He has been able to build and maintain strong relationships with funders, strategic
customers, supply chain partners, and government agencies.
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only
4

raw_text:
Qatar Advantage
• Qatar provides a strategic location with local access to the world’s newest greenfield deep-seaport and is ideally situated for
importing raw materials and exporting battery chemicals to target markets with it already committing to chemical and metals
processing as part of its Third Development Strategy under the Qatar National Vision 20301 (QNV2030).
• As a respected global financial partner, the Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) launched its first venture capital Fund of Funds as a
strategic investment program designed as a catalyst to further strengthen the venture capital landscape in Qatar by attracting leading
venture capital funds and entrepreneurs. The program will invest more than USD 1 billion in international and regional venture capital
funds and focus on nurturing a robust venture capital ecosystem. The fund will help boost economic diversification, target sector
growth, support local talent development, and promote sustainability across the State of Qatar.
• The Qatar Free Zones Authority (QFZ) is an independent authority created in 2018 to oversee and regulate the world-class free
zones in Qatar, offering outstanding opportunities and benefits for businesses seeking to expand their global operations which is
strong impetus for Low-Qem investing in Qatar.
• Energy supply from Qatar Energy will comfortably provide Low-Qem with the energy security it needs and, considering the
competitive energy pricing in the area, this provides strong incentive for investment in such a project in Qatar.
• Qatar boasts world-class academia and pioneering research institutions, and its government is committed to promoting innovation
and nurturing the next generation of Qatari scientists and engineers which makes it the perfect place to grow the next generation of
minerologists and process engineers.
1 – Published on the Third Qatar National Development Strategy 2024–2030 in 2022
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only
5

raw_text:
Regional Importance
Shaped by the aspirations and guiding principles in QNV2030, The Third National Development Strategy (NDS3) for the State of Qatar establishes the strategic
priorities and overarching framework for the next period of the nation’s development from 2024-2030. NDS3 outlines the major outcomes and targets, along with the
development initiatives and reforms, that are necessary to achieve the aspirations of QNV 2030.
One of those outcomes is a new manufacturing cluster that includes a goal to increase chemical and low-carbon metals processing.

raw_text:
Market Overview
There is a chronic under-investment in the new production of battery grade products outside China, and this is driving structural deficit. However, Qatar is seeing this as
an opportunity to capitalize on its own diversification ambitions. Aside from general economic diversification, Qatar has the foresight to identify chemicals and low-
carbon metals as a core area of development.
All the while policy-driven demand for battery materials, including CAM, is rapidly increasing due to the push towards electric vehicles globally. The combination of these
factors will create a significant shortage in the coming decade and the while raw material supplies are forecasted to exceed demand, the main supply chain shortfall is in
refining and conversion capacity to convert feedstock into usable, battery-grade chemicals.
Hence, Qatar is thinking—and more importantly—acting strategically by moving at pace to support new ventures like Low-Qem within its economic fabric and
infrastructure.
Global lithium supply-demand imbalance1 Investment benefits: Qatar’s commitment to expanding critical minerals
processing:
1. Counter-Cyclical Investment: Investing during a
downturn positions companies to benefit from future Qatar’s Third National Development Strategy has a specific
price rebounds emphasis on expanding manufacturing in chemicals and
low-carbon metals as part of it’s core Manufacturing
2. Long-term demand (very) bullish: Investing in lithium
during price downturns ensures long-term profitability, cluster.
supply security, and competitive advantage.
"Through NDS3, Qatar transitions into the next phase of its
3. Price recovery to be significant: Lithium can skyrocket
development, shifting towards a sustainable economic
when demand outpaces supply, like 2021–2022 when
lithium reached over $80,000 per ton. future driven by its citizens, residents, and the private
sector, while maintaining our values, identity, and social
4. Securing raw materials before demand spikes: tier 1 fabric.“
customers are positioning themselves to control their
own lithium supply, reducing dependence on volatile
markets. -H.E. Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al-
5. Build an integrated supply chain: Low pricing Thani
encourages auto and battery manufacturers to enter
long-term supply agreements, securing future revenue. (Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs)
1 – Based on the European Commission’s Raw Materials Information System (RMIS), titled Lithium-based batteries supply chain challenges.
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only

raw_text:
B2B Value Chain
Low-Qem has extensive expertise and know how in the critical minerals and battery sector(s) with experience in upstream, midstream and
downstream processing of minerals, metals and chemicals for global markets.
Considering the global supply shortage across mid-stream processing and conversion, incoming investors will have the opportunity to expand their investment footprint across the
value chain. Initial exposure will also include supply and offtake agreements with other key parts of the value chain such as mine supplier and chemical offtakers.
MINE SUPPLY CHEMICAL CAM / PCAM LITHIUM-ION ELECTRIC
CONVERSION BATTERY CELLS VEHICLES
.
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only
8

raw_text:
Project Overview
• Initially Low-Qem will focus its development in Qatar on the processing of battery products such as lithium. Key Data based on a Lithium plant (all figures in $m [USD])
Lithium production • 50ktpa Lithium salt (LiOH or Li2CO3 )
• The lithium process is based on hydrometallurgical conversion which includes calcining, pressure leaching, Used in cathode active material production
conversion, ion exchange and crystallization process stages.
Byproduct • 510ktpa Analcime (NaAlSi2O6·H2O)
Used as cement replacement product
• Chosen technologies would result in a compact footprint lithium production plant to produce the target
Other products • Technical support and R&D services
battery grade lithium products.
Feedstock (380ktpa) • 340ktpa Spodumene Concentrate (6% Li2O)
• Processing and conditioning the 510,000tpa analcime output could result in a 317,000tpa CO2 reduction, • 220ktpa Other reagents
resulting in a meaningful carbon offset by replacing virgin cement in the construction industries. Eg. Sodium carbonate (“Soda Ash”) and calcium oxide (“Quicklime”)
Trains • 25ktpa train 1
Commercialised Lithium Hydroxide process flowsheet1 • 25ktpa train 2
Land Take • 250,000m2
Utilities • Plant gas: 3290Nm3/h
• Plant electricity: 14980kW
• Process Gases: Nitrogen, Instrument Air, CO2
Project Cost • $900m capex
(incl. inc. $150m working capital)
Timing • Development Phase 2025-2026
• Construction Phase 2027-2029
• Operational Phase 2030 onwards
Project Life • 30 years
• +20 years extension
Jobs • 2,000 Construction Jobs
• 500 Operational Jobs
1 – Metso’s direct leach process offering a sulfate and acid-free method to produce battery-grade lithium hydroxide monohydrate from spodumene concentrates. The process
is environmentally sustainable as the main mineral residue “Analcime sand” is inert and readily neutralized.
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only
9

raw_text:
Lower Carbon Pledge
Our 4-point ‘Lower Carbon Pledge’ will deliver a 75% reduction vs. Chinese refineries’ current emissions, achievable by
initiatives to eliminate Scope 1 emissions and materially reduce residual Scopes 2 & 3 emissions
1 We will target emissions below 4.0 tCO2/tLiOH. This compares to a Chinese emissions average of 16.2 tCO2/tLiOH and is enabled by
leveraging clean energy such as solar and hydrogen infrastructure.
2 We will tap into unique Qatar-based CCUS and Hydrogen programs, and wider green energy infrastructure such as solar, renewable
electricity, etc. to produce lithium at 25% the CO2 footprint of existing Chinese operators.
We will do everything we can to generate up to 5.0 tCO2/tLiOH in credits against refinery emissions, whilst creating a profitable – and IP
3
rich – secondary revenue stream, by re-processing our byproduct as a low-carbon construction material. Another benefit of our
process.
We will deploy an alkali-leach flowsheet that eliminates resulting fume emissions, removes acid residues in process, and reduces
4
acid handling on site. Chinese operators use power-intensive acid roast processing and with carbon-intensive energy sources. This is
something we will never adopt.
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only
10
cleaned_text:
Lower Carbon Pledge
Our 4-point ‘Lower Carbon Pledge’ will deliver a 75% reduction vs. Chinese refineries’ current emissions, achievable by
initiatives to eliminate Scope 1 emissions and materially reduce residual Scopes 2 & 3 emissions
1 We will target emissions below 4.0 tCO2/tLiOH. This compares to a Chinese emissions average of 16.2 tCO2/tLiOH and is enabled by
leveraging clean energy such as solar and hydrogen infrastructure.
2 We will tap into unique Qatar-based CCUS and Hydrogen programs, and wider green energy infrastructure such as solar, renewable
electricity, etc. to produce lithium at 25% the CO2 footprint of existing Chinese operators.
We will do everything we can to generate up to 5.0 tCO2/tLiOH in credits against refinery emissions, whilst creating a profitable – and IP
rich – secondary revenue stream, by re-processing our byproduct as a low-carbon construction material. Another benefit of our
process.
We will deploy an alkali-leach flowsheet that eliminates resulting fume emissions, removes acid residues in process, and reduces
acid handling on site. Chinese operators use power-intensive acid roast processing and with carbon-intensive energy sources. This is
something we will never adopt.

raw_text:
$2m Investment Timeline
$2m in seed funding—over 3 distinctive tranches—will enable Low-Qem to deliver major project milestones that will offer an incremental valuation uplift to existing shareholders.
The project team will be planning, organizing and delivering major millstones that will then enable the Series A and Series B investment process to commence in 2026, before preparing
for construction finance in late 2027.
Strategic business development will be ongoing throughout the project lifecycle to ensure that key supply and offtake agreements can be negotiated to enable project financing in 2027.
2025 2026 2027
Q2 Q3 Q4 Q1 Q2 Q3 Q4 Q1 Q2
Seed investment received $500k $1m $500k
Tranche 1 Tranche 2 Tranche 3
Key people onboarded
Launch strategic business
development
Feedstock sample received
Flowsheet Testwork
Feasibility study & report
Lifecycle carbon assessment
Launch ‘Series A’ funding
Private and Confidential: For Limited Circulation Only
